summary: Refactor LCS Spine to CTB Cantonal pattern in Neon via migration-only changes: add one CANONICAL table and one ERROR table, expose doctrinal INTAKE surface as lcs.lcs_intake (view over existing cid_intake), retain CID/SID/MID supporting mechanics and existing append-only/FSM enforcement, update DB functions to write canonical state and log failures, and register all LCS tables in CTB registry using existing repo leaf taxonomy.

payload.objective: Refactor LCS to strict CTB Cantonal compliance by introducing INTAKE + single CANONICAL + single ERROR surfaces while retaining CID/SID/MID supporting state/log layers and DB-minted MID flow.

classification.rationale: Refactor within existing backbone and protected models. The work applies the inherited Cantonal pattern to the local lcs schema without modifying CTB backbone primitives, altitude hierarchy, sovereign ID doctrine, cantonal/ERROR definitions, registry enforcement model, or constitutional artifacts.

in_scope: Create CANONICAL table lcs.lcs_canonical (single authoritative row per sovereign_id)
Create ERROR table lcs.lcs_errors (cantonal error capture)
Expose doctrinal INTAKE surface as lcs.lcs_intake using Option 2 (view over lcs.cid_intake) to preserve v0 table and enforcement safely
Update lcs.fn_process_cid(p_cid uuid) to validate CID, ensure/UPSERT canonical row, update canonical state fields, preserve DB-minted MID and forward-only dispatch enforcement
Update lcs.fn_finalize_dispatch(p_mid uuid, p_result jsonb) to update terminal MID dispatch state, reflect canonical dispatch state/current_mid as applicable, and log failure paths to lcs.lcs_errors
Add CTB registry entries for all LCS tables using the correct registry table and existing repo leaf taxonomy
out_of_scope: Non-migration code changes unless strictly required for schema registration (prefer migration-only)

canonical_table.name: lcs.lcs_canonical
requirements: One row per sovereign_id (PK + FK to cl.company_identity(company_unique_id))
Columns include current_lifecycle_stage, current_sid, current_mid, current_dispatch_state, last_cid, created_at, updated_at
FKs to lcs.sid_registry(sid), lcs.mid_ledger(mid), and intake CID primary key
updated_at auto-updates on allowed state transitions
Use repo-consistent trigger guard pattern so updates are only allowed through LCS functions (enforcement skeleton acceptable in v1)

error_table.name: lcs.lcs_errors
requirements: Append-only except resolved_at may be set
Columns include error_id, error_stage, error_type, retry_strategy, sovereign_id, cid, sid, mid, error_payload, created_at, resolved_at
Applicable FK constraints to cl.company_identity, lcs.cid_intake (or underlying intake table), lcs.sid_registry, lcs.mid_ledger
Failure paths in LCS functions must insert deterministic error rows

intake_surface.implementation_choice: Option 2: create view mapping to existing lcs.cid_intake
intake_surface.rationale: Safest path that preserves v0 table, append-only enforcement, and compatibility while providing doctrinal INTAKE naming surface required by CTB alignment.
invariants: Deprecate safely via doctrinal surface instead of destructive rename

registry_requirements.action: Register all LCS tables in existing CTB registry table used by this repo via migration
rules: Do not invent a new registry table name; use repo-existing CTB registry table/schema
Use existing repo leaf type taxonomy and map requested roles (INTAKE/CANONICAL/ERROR/REGISTRY/LEDGER-HISTORY) to valid local leaf types
Include entries for newly added lcs.lcs_canonical and lcs.lcs_errors and supporting lcs tables retained/used in v1

acceptance_criteria: lcs.lcs_canonical exists and enforces one row per sovereign_id
lcs.lcs_errors exists, is append-only (except resolved_at), and captures function failure events
A doctrinal intake surface exists as lcs.lcs_intake and remains append-only through underlying enforcement
lcs.fn_process_cid() deterministically updates lcs.lcs_canonical on success
lcs.fn_finalize_dispatch() updates canonical dispatch state and logs failures to lcs.lcs_errors
CTB registry contains entries for all LCS tables with appropriate valid repo leaf types
Auditor can verify no scope violations, objects exist, functions are callable, and canonical row updates occur
operator_smoke_tests: Insert intake event (CID PENDING), process CID, verify MID minted, SID exists/upserted, and canonical row reflects last_cid/current_mid/current_sid/current_dispatch_state
Force error path (e.g., invalid movement_type_code or invalid CID status) and verify lcs.lcs_errors row is written
Verify append-only constraints still block illegal updates

deliverables: Migration SQL implementing CTB cantonal refactor for lcs schema in a transaction
CHANGESET artifact referencing migration path(s) only
